title: thymus pulegioides var. praeflorens
intro: Thymus pulegioides var. praeflorens, also known as wild thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus pulegioides plant, a low-growing, fragrant perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Thymus pulegioides var. praeflorens include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective against various infections and diseases. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to boost the immune system, relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and act as a natural antihistamine to reduce allergic reactions. Thymus pulegioides var. praeflorens can be prepared as a tea, oil, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
